rationale: The hypercasual "relaxing satisfaction" audience overlaps strongly with Push'em All's broad casual base, but the hook itself is fundamentally mismatched: traffic-jam puzzle solving is slow/cerebral while Push'em All's value prop is energetic crowd-pushing physics chaos — there's no honest way to bait this hook and deliver the actual game without a bounce. Palette and stylus-on-vehicles visuals also clash with the stickman-on-platform 3D look. Recommend skipping a direct adaptation; if testing, only borrow the "have you played a game that..." VO framing over native push-crowds-off-platform footage rather than replicating the traffic-jam hook.
